right_target.finding: OpenTargets returns 42 evidence rows linking SETD2 to melanoma; Cancer Gene Census scores SETD2 as a somatic driver (score 1.0) in melanoma and cutaneous melanoma, plus uveal (0.75) and acral lentiginous (0.75) melanoma, backed by 20+ literature refs (e.g. PMIDs 28481359, 26286987). SETD2 is a recurrently-mutated chromatin regulator, not a validated actionable driver, so genetic causality is real but modest. gwas_get_snps_for_gene returns 3 unique locus SNPs (rs61734084 synonymous, rs7633698 & rs11707736 intronic) with NO melanoma trait association.
right_target.provenance: OpenTargets_get_evidence_by_datasource (MONDO_0005105, 42 rows, cancer_gene_census score 1.0); gwas_get_snps_for_gene (3 SNPs, no melanoma trait)

right_tissue.finding: CellMarker lists SETD2 as a marker in only 3 human cell contexts, all normal tissue (meiotic-prophase fetal germ cell; fetal-kidney NKT cell; pancreatic-islet alpha cell), 1 support each via single-cell sequencing. No melanocyte or skin/melanoma tissue localization is reported, so tissue rationale for melanoma is absent.
right_tissue.provenance: CellMarker_search_by_gene (SETD2, Human, total_records=3, all Normal cell)

right_safety.finding: CellMarker normal-cell expression spans fetal germ cell, fetal-kidney NKT cell and pancreatic-islet alpha cell (3 normal cell types), implying essential/broad chromatin-regulator roles and on-target toxicity risk. No SETD2-directed drug exists (see commercial), so OpenFDA_search_drug_events is not applicable and no post-market adverse-event signal could be queried.
right_safety.provenance: CellMarker_search_by_gene (3 normal cell types); OpenFDA not applicable (no SETD2 drug in clinic)

right_patient.finding: search_clinical_trials (intervention=SETD2, condition=melanoma) returned an explicit no-studies-found error. No SETD2-targeted trial and no patient-stratification biomarker strategy exists in melanoma.
right_patient.provenance: search_clinical_trials (intervention=SETD2, condition=melanoma) -> error: No studies found

right_commercial.finding: No commercial asset: search_clinical_trials found 0 SETD2 melanoma trials, and TheraSAbDab_search_by_target returned 0 therapeutic antibodies (count=0). No SETD2 drug-in-clinic for melanoma.

tractability.finding: Pharos classifies SETD2 as Tchem (small-molecule ligand exists / chemically probed but not clinical-grade), UniProt Q9BYW2, Epigenetic family histone-lysine N-methyltransferase (H3K36), novelty 0.0018 (well-studied, 142 publications). Tchem indicates chemical starting points but no approved/clinical drug; as a large multidomain chromatin methyltransferase it is a hard target.
tractability.provenance: Pharos_get_target (gene=SETD2, tdl=Tchem, fam=Epigenetic, uniprot Q9BYW2, publicationCount=142)
